exports.name: dateTime
exports.signature: dateTime(opt?: { input?: DateTimeInput; format?: FormatInput; timeZone?: TimeZone; lang?: string }): DateTime
exports.description: Creates a DateTime instance. Accepts optional input to parse, a strict format string, a time zone, and a locale lang. With no arguments returns current time in the default time zone.

parameters.name: opt
parameters.type: { input?: DateTimeInput; format?: FormatInput; timeZone?: TimeZone; lang?: string }
parameters.description: Options object. input: value to parse (string, number, Date, array, object, DateTime, null, undefined). format: strict dayjs format string (FormatInput = string | undefined; not re-exported from the package index). timeZone: IANA zone, 'utc', or 'browser'. lang: locale code.

exports.name: dateTimeUtc
exports.signature: dateTimeUtc(opt?: { input?: DateTimeInput; format?: FormatInput; lang?: string; offset?: number }): DateTime
exports.description: Creates a DateTime instance with a fixed UTC offset instead of a named time zone. The offset defaults to 0 (UTC).

parameters.name: opt
parameters.type: { input?: DateTimeInput; format?: FormatInput; lang?: string; offset?: number }
parameters.description: Options object. input: value to parse. format: strict dayjs format string (FormatInput = string | undefined; not re-exported from the package index). lang: locale code. offset: UTC offset in minutes (default 0).

exports.name: isDateTime
exports.description: Checks if a value is a DateTime instance.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: Value to check.

exports.name: expandFormat
exports.description: Expands localized format tokens (L, LL, LLL, LLLL, LT, LTS and their lowercase variants) in a format string into their full representations using the provided or current locale's long date format map.

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: Format string potentially containing localized tokens.

parameters.name: formats
parameters.description: Long date format map. Defaults to the current locale's formats.

exports.name: dateTimeParse
exports.signature: dateTimeParse(input: unknown, options?: DateTimeOptionsWhenParsing): DateTime | undefined
exports.description: Parses a number, text, or Date to a DateTime value. Supports absolute dates and relative date-math expressions such as 'now-6h' or 'now/d'. Returns undefined for invalid or empty input.

parameters.name: input
parameters.description: Value to parse: string, number, Date, array, object, DateTime, or null/undefined.

parameters.name: options
parameters.description: Options: timeZone (IANA zone / 'utc' / 'browser'), format (strict parse format), lang (locale), roundUp (round the end of relative ranges up), allowRelative (default true; set false to reject relative expressions). Note: DateTimeOptionsWhenParsing is not re-exported from the package index and cannot be directly imported.

exports.name: isValid
exports.description: Checks whether a value is a valid date. Accepts a DateTime instance or a string parseable by dateTimeParse (including relative expressions).

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: Value to validate.

exports.name: isLikeRelative
exports.description: Returns true if the value looks like a relative date expression (e.g. 'now', 'now-1d'). Uses the currently configured relative parser.

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: Value to test.

exports.name: defaultRelativeParse
exports.signature: defaultRelativeParse(text: string, options?: { roundUp?: boolean; timeZone?: TimeZone }): DateTime | undefined
exports.description: The built-in relative date-math parser. Parses expressions like 'now', 'now-6h', 'now/d', 'now+1d/d'. Exported as the default implementation used by dateTimeParse.

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: Relative date expression to parse.

parameters.name: options
parameters.description: roundUp: round to end of unit instead of start. timeZone: override time zone.

exports.name: defaultIsLikeRelative
exports.description: The built-in implementation of isLikeRelative. Returns true when the string starts with 'now'.

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: String to test.

exports.name: getTimeZonesList
exports.description: Returns all available IANA time zone identifiers using Intl.supportedValuesOf('timeZone'). Returns an empty array if the runtime does not support the API.

exports.name: guessUserTimeZone
exports.description: Returns the user's local IANA time zone identifier using Intl.DateTimeFormat().resolvedOptions().timeZone.

exports.name: isValidTimeZone
exports.description: Checks whether a string is a valid IANA time zone identifier. Results are cached internally.

parameters.name: zone
parameters.description: Time zone identifier to validate.

exports.name: timeZoneOffset
exports.description: Returns the UTC offset in minutes for a given time zone at a specific Unix timestamp (milliseconds). Returns NaN for invalid zones or timestamps.

parameters.name: zone
parameters.description: IANA time zone, 'utc', 'browser', or 'system'.

parameters.name: ts
parameters.description: Unix timestamp in milliseconds.

exports.name: duration
exports.signature: duration(amount: DurationInput, unit?: DurationUnit, options?: { lang?: string }): Duration
exports.description: Creates a Duration instance. Accepts a number (with optional unit), an ISO 8601 duration string (e.g. 'P1Y2M3DT4H'), a duration input object, or an existing Duration. Throws on unrecognised input.

parameters.name: amount
parameters.description: Amount as number, ISO 8601 string, object, or Duration.

parameters.name: unit
parameters.description: Unit for numeric amount (e.g. 'hours', 'days'). Defaults to 'milliseconds'.

parameters.name: options
parameters.description: locale code for humanization.

exports.name: duration
exports.signature: duration(amount: DurationInput, options?: { lang?: string }): Duration
exports.description: Creates a Duration instance. Overload that accepts options directly as the second argument without a unit. Accepts a number, an ISO 8601 duration string (e.g. 'P1Y2M3DT4H'), a duration input object, or an existing Duration. Throws on unrecognised input.

parameters.name: amount
parameters.description: Amount as number, ISO 8601 string, object, or Duration.

parameters.name: options
parameters.description: Options object. lang: locale code for humanization.

exports.name: isDuration
exports.description: Checks whether a value is a Duration instance.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: Value to check.

exports.name: settings
exports.description: Singleton settings object that manages global configuration for all DateTime instances. Supports locale loading/setting, default time zone configuration, and custom relative parser injection.

exports.name: UtcTimeZone
exports.description: Constant string 'UTC' representing the UTC time zone, for use as a timeZone argument.

exports.name: HTML5_INPUT_FORMATS
exports.signature: HTML5_INPUT_FORMATS: { DATETIME_LOCAL: string; DATETIME_LOCAL_SECONDS: string; DATETIME_LOCAL_MS: string; DATE: string; TIME: string; TIME_SECONDS: string; TIME_MS: string; WEEK: string; MONTH: string }
exports.description: Format string constants matching HTML5 date/time input element formats. Keys: DATETIME_LOCAL, DATETIME_LOCAL_SECONDS, DATETIME_LOCAL_MS, DATE, TIME, TIME_SECONDS, TIME_MS, WEEK, MONTH.

exports.name: DateTime
exports.description: Immutable date-time object returned by dateTime(), dateTimeUtc(), and dateTimeParse(). Provides chainable methods for arithmetic, formatting, comparison, zone switching, and all getter/setter operations for every date-time component.

exports.name: DateTimeInput
exports.signature: type DateTimeInput = InputObject | Date | string | number | Array<string | number> | DateTime | null | undefined
exports.description: Union type for all values accepted as date-time input by dateTime() and dateTimeParse(). Covers plain objects, native Date, ISO strings, Unix timestamps, year-month-day arrays, DateTime instances, and nullish values.

exports.name: Duration
exports.description: Immutable duration object returned by duration(). Provides getters (milliseconds, seconds, minutes, hours, days, weeks, months, quarters, years), as*() total-length methods, arithmetic (add, subtract, negate), conversion (shiftTo, rescale, normalize), and formatting (format, humanize, humanizeIntl, toISOString).

exports.name: DurationInput
exports.signature: type DurationInput = Duration | number | string | DurationInputObject | null | undefined
exports.description: Union type for all values accepted as duration input by duration() and DateTime arithmetic methods.

exports.name: DurationUnit
exports.description: Union of all valid duration unit strings: year/years/y, month/months/M, day/days/d, hour/hours/h, minute/minutes/m, second/seconds/s, millisecond/milliseconds/ms, quarter/quarters/Q, week/weeks/w.
